front: Building: 354 prefabricated 90-ton concrete boxes stacked into 158 apartments near the St. Lawrence River, built for Montreal's Expo 67

Building: this Montreal housing complex grew from its architect's master's thesis 'A Case for City Living' and was guided by the motto 'For Everyone a Garden,' with each roof serving as another unit's garden
Building: professor Sandy Van Ginkel convinced its architect to submit his master's thesis to a design contest; the resulting project was reduced from a planned 1,000 units
Building: August Komendant supervised the structural engineering of this Montreal residential complex of stacked concrete boxes
Building: this Montreal housing complex resembles Ricardo Bofill's Kafka Castle and inspired the Nakagin Capsule Tower
Building: Jerry Adamson designed special rotary-moulded polyethylene armchairs specifically for this stacked-concrete-box housing complex
Building: a planned extension of this housing complex to the Virgin Islands was designed to 'root like a cactus to a rocky peninsula'; the book For Everyone a Garden documents its extension to Israel
Building: this Singapore resort's three towers — shaped like a deck of cards — are connected at their tops by a 340-meter SkyPark containing the world's largest infinity pool
Building: this Singapore resort designed by Moshe Safdie appears prominently in the 2018 film Crazy Rich Asians
Building: the coffee table book Reaching for the Sky documents the construction of the world's largest infinity pool in this Singapore resort
Building: commissioned by Walmart heiress Alice Walton; Safdie dammed a river to create two pools dividing eight shell-like pavilions, with two buildings acting as bridges over the pond
back: Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art (Moshe Safdie)
work: Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art

front: Building: this Bentonville, Arkansas museum with arched copper roofs owns Asher Durand's landscape painting Kindred Spirits
back: Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art (Moshe Safdie)
work: Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art

front: Building: this Kansas City performing arts center features symmetrical half-shells of concentric vertical arches and resembles a pair of connected sliced-up onions from the back
back: Kauffmann Center for the Performing Arts (Moshe Safdie)
work: Kauffmann Center for the Performing Arts

front: Architect: this Israeli-Canadian architect apprenticed with Louis Kahn
Architect: this architect designed the Yad Vashem Holocaust memorial museum in Jerusalem, opened in 2005
Architect: this architect designed the Yitzhak Rabin Center in Tel Aviv
Architect: this architect designed the Jewel Changi airport terminal in Singapore — a five-story indoor garden of tropical trees and shrubs
Architect: this architect designed the National Gallery of Canada
Architect: this architect designed the United States Institute of Peace, whose white roof resembles a dove's wing
Architect: this architect designed the headquarters of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives
